title: Arumeru District Council (Secondary School Compulsory Attendance) By-laws, 2003
title_en: Arumeru District Council (Secondary School Compulsory Attendance) By-laws, 2003

provisions.content: These By-laws, shall be cited as the Arumeru District Council (Secondary School Compulsory Attendance) By-laws 2003 and shall come into operation on the day of publication.
These By-laws shall apply to the whole area of jurisdiction of the Arumeru District Council.

In these By-laws " Orphan " means a student whose parents or guardians have died; " Parent " means any resident of Arumeru District who is the father, mother or guardian of the student attending secondary education in government school within the United Republic of Tanzania; " School " means government schools within the United Republic of Tanzania; " Student " means a person who is attending secondary education in Government school within the United Republic of Tanzania.

All parents shall pay yearly fees to the school of which his or her student is attending according to the manner which the school in which the student is attending will prescribe until the completion of secondary education.
District council may, in its discretion pay wholly or part of school fees provided that Student is proved to be orphan.

Any parent who fails to pay the fees prescribed by the school, shall be liable for conviction to a fine not exceeding three hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for a term not exceeding twelve month or to both fine and imprisonment.

Any person other than parent, who under any circumstances, cause a student not to attend school regularly until the completion of secondary education shall be guilty of an offence and shall be liable for conviction to a fine not exceeding three hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for a term not exceeding twelve months or to both fine and imprisonment.

Any person who give false information which may cause the council to believes that a student is an orphan, shall be guilty of an offence upon found that the student is not orphan, and shall be liable on conviction to a fine not exceeding three hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for a term not exceeding twelve months or to both fine and imprisonment.
